name: Hiking from Marín — circular route through Monte Penizas and the Lameira river
name_original: Mogor desde Marín — circular por el Monte Penizas y el río Lameira
region_intro: Hiking around Marín offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal paths with forest trails and mountain ascents. The region features varied terrain, from the beaches along the Ría de Pontevedra to the hills of Monte Penizas. Hikers can explore ancient sites like the Mámoa do Rei burial mound and enjoy views from numerous viewpoints. The Lameira River and Lake Castiñeiras provide additional natural settings for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Marín The most popular hiking route is Hiking from Marín — circular route through Monte Penizas and the Lameira river, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends Monte Penizas, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Playa de Mogor – Mogor Petroglyphs loop from Marín, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail combines coastal walking with visits to ancient petroglyphs near Mogor Beach. Local hikers also love the Fonte de Bastarreche – Marín Promenade loop from Marín, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas and along the promenade, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Marín is defined by coastal paths, forest trails, and viewpoints overlooking the estuary. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Marín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Marín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Marín?
A: Marín offers a diverse selection of over 18 hiking routes. These include 6 easy trails, 10 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Marín?
A: Yes, Marín has 6 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mogor Petroglyphs – Playa de Mogor loop from Praia do Moscoso&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.3 km route that combines coastal scenery with historical sites. The 'Route of the Burial Mounds' around Lake Castiñeiras is also noted for its moderate slopes, making it suitable for families.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Marín?
A: Yes, many of Marín's hiking trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375644891/hiking-from-marin-circular-route-through-monte-penizas-and-the-lameira-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking from Marín — circular route through Monte Penizas and the Lameira river&lt;/a&gt;, the 'Route of the Burial Mounds', and the 'Route of the Five Viewpoints'.

Q: Are there any hiking trails in Marín that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, Marín offers trails where you can discover waterfalls. The Fervenza de Neibó, a small waterfall in Santo Tomé de Piñeiro, is a beautiful natural sight within a leafy grove. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza da Moura&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the 'Waterfalls around Marín' guide.

Q: What kind of historical or archaeological sites can I see while hiking in Marín?
A: Marín is rich in historical and archaeological sites. You can visit the ancient burial mounds ('mámoas') around Lake Castiñeiras, including the Mámoa do Rei. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19881103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Mogor – Mogor Petroglyphs loop from Marín&lt;/a&gt; takes you to the Mogor Petroglyphs, ancient rock carvings. The 'Route of the Five Viewpoints' also allows visits to the Castro da Subidá, a fortified settlement.

Q: Are there hiking routes in Marín with panoramic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Marín is known for its stunning viewpoints. Monte Penizas offers spectacular panoramic views over Marín and the Ría de Pontevedra. The 'Route of the Five Viewpoints' specifically connects panoramic points like Mogor, Cruceiro do Vento, Moreira, Portocelo, and Barrio, providing extensive vistas of the Pontevedra estuary.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Marín?
A: The hiking experience in Marín is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths and beaches to forest trails and mountain ascents, as well as the impressive views from numerous viewpoints overlooking the estuary.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking trails in Marín?
A: Many trails in Marín are suitable for hiking with dogs, especially those through natural areas like forests and along coastal paths. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific beaches or protected zones.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Marín?
A: Marín offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for exploring. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal walks where you can combine hiking with a refreshing dip in the sea. Winter hiking is possible, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler or wetter.

Q: Are there any attractions or points of interest near the hiking trails in Marín?
A: Yes, Marín's hiking trails are often close to various attractions. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/450468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/488410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tomeza Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and traditional Galician Hórreos. The area also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Pilgrim Virgin&lt;/a&gt; and the Redondela Historic Center, offering cultural insights alongside your outdoor adventures.

Q: Are there hiking routes that combine mountains and beaches in Marín?
A: Yes, Marín is unique in its blend of coastal and mountainous terrain. The 'Mountain and Beach Route' is a circular option that connects the municipality's main natural sites, traversing beaches like Portocelo, Mogor, and Aguete, as well as rural areas and trails with spectacular views, showcasing the contrast between the coast and the interior.
tips: To the south of the Pontevedra Marín neighborhood you reach this magical sandy beach with beautiful views of the Rías Baixas.
Summit Monte Penizas to obtain a spectacular view over Marín and the Ría de Pontevedra. Forest trails lead to the top.
Follow the Lameira River from Marín to begin the gentle ascent towards Monte Penizas Park. Enjoy the peaceful river scenery and listen to the bird.

waypoints.name: View of Marín and the Ría de Pontevedra from Monte Penizas
